title: Server Configuration, HTTP/1.1, HTTP/2, TLS, and Graceful Shutdown · Language Lens: Server Timeout Header Limits
summary: A public server uses package defaults, has zero timeouts, trusts unlimited headers, enables cleartext HTTP/2 accidentally, serves weak TLS, and terminates active requests on deploy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo server timeout header limits increment in explicit http.Server protocol and shutdown configuration. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
Reserve cleanup time, release rows and transactions, compensate partial external effects, and verify no work outlives its intended authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Server Timeout Header Limits · Theory Server Timeout Header Limits
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 9337 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate HTTP/1.1 and HTTP/2 service by timeout and graceful-drain policy while excluding HTTP/3; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Configure ReadHeaderTimeout, body strategy, WriteTimeout, IdleTimeout, MaxHeaderBytes, and per-handler deadlines from threat and workload evidence.
